# BPMfinder — Free Online BPM Tempo Analyzer

> Detect the BPM (beats per minute) of any song instantly in your browser. 100% private — all processing happens client-side via essentia.js WASM. No servers, no sign-ups, no data uploads.

## Input Modes

- **File Upload**: Drop or click to browse audio files (MP3, WAV, FLAC, OGG, OPUS, AAC, M4A). Max 200MB. Auto-analyzes on file select.
- **YouTube URL**: Paste a YouTube link (youtube.com/watch?v=, youtu.be/, shorts/, embed/) to analyze audio via Piped API. Falls back to Tab Capture if Piped is unavailable.
- **Microphone Capture**: Click "Microphone" in the Capture tab to record 10 seconds of live audio via your device mic (requires HTTPS/localhost).
- **Tab Capture**: Click "Tab Audio" in the Capture tab to capture 10 seconds of audio from another browser tab via getDisplayMedia (requires Chrome 74+, Firefox 76+, Safari 17+).

## Features

- BPM display with animated counter and color-coded confidence indicator (green ≥70%, amber ≥50%, red <50%)
- Tempo classification: Grave (1-40), Largo (41-60), Larghetto (61-66), Adagio (67-76), Andante (77-108), Moderato (109-120), Allegro (121-156), Vivace (157-176), Presto (177-200), Prestissimo (201+)
- Tempo reference modal with descriptions for each classification
- Dark and light theme (persisted in localStorage)
- Drag-and-drop support for file upload
- Fully responsive: mobile, tablet, desktop
- Offline support for file analysis via Service Worker
- Keyboard accessible: Tab navigation, Enter to submit

## How to Use

1. Choose input mode: File, YouTube, or Capture (tabs at top of page)
2. For File: drag an audio file onto the drop zone or click to browse
3. For YouTube: paste a YouTube URL and click Analyze
4. For Capture: choose Microphone or Tab Audio, grant permission when prompted
5. Wait ~1-2 seconds for analysis — BPM result appears with animation
6. Click ⓘ next to the tempo label to see the full tempo reference table
7. Toggle dark/light theme with the sun/moon icon in the header

## Technical Details

- **BPM Detection**: essentia.js WASM (RhythmExtractor2013 algorithm) from MTG/UPF
- **YouTube Metadata**: Piped API (public instances, no API key needed)
- **Audio Capture**: WebRTC APIs (getUserMedia for mic, getDisplayMedia for tab)
- **Audio Codecs**: Web Audio API decodes MP3, WAV, FLAC, OGG, OPUS, AAC
- **Offline**: Service Worker caches essentia.js WASM and static assets
- **No Backend**: 100% static site, deployed on GitHub Pages
